topic: A student finds out their scholarship was meant for someone else
raw_output:
[story 1]

The official letter lay crumpled on the desk, the ink stark and unforgiving. Liam stared at the lines, his knuckles white where he gripped the edge of the wood. It wasn't just a misplaced name; it was a complete redirection. He reread the paragraph about the financial aid, the precise wording that confirmed his entire academic trajectory was built on a fundamental misunderstanding. A slow, hot pressure began to build behind his eyes, a tightening in his chest that made every breath feel shallow. He pushed the papers away with unnecessary force, the sound sharp in the quiet dorm room. He stood up, pacing the small space, his movements jerky and agitated, until he was running a hand roughly through his hair, trying to physically shake the unsettling feeling loose.

[story 2]

"This is impossible," Maya whispered, the words catching in her throat like shards of glass. She kept turning the acceptance documentation over and over in her hands, the glossy paper feeling slick and mocking. The realization hit her with a physical jolt—a sudden, cold emptiness where hope used to reside. Her jaw clenched so tightly she could feel the strain in her molars. She slammed the folder shut, the sound echoing loudly in the otherwise silent library corner. She stood up abruptly, knocking her chair slightly askew. Her gaze swept over the rows of textbooks, landing on a particularly dusty volume, and she snatched it up, flipping through the pages with furious, rapid strokes, her breathing becoming shallow and quick.

[story 3]

He paced the length of the deserted campus quad, the late afternoon sun casting long, distorted shadows behind him. Every step was heavy, deliberate, fueled by a churning resentment that made his stomach clench painfully. He stopped abruptly beneath a sprawling oak, his chest heaving. He looked down at his hands, flexing his fingers until the tendons stood out starkly against his skin. A low, guttural sound escaped his throat—a sound of pure, contained frustration. He clenched his fists so tightly that his nails bit into his palms, his entire body vibrating with an intense, restless energy that demanded release. He turned away from the campus, walking with a stiff, determined stride toward the nearest exit, refusing to look back at the building that held the answer.
